scenario_name: FIFA World Cup Qatar 2022 Boycott Debate
description: The 2022 World Cup in Qatar generated sustained controversy over migrant worker deaths, LGBTQ+ rights, and corruption in the bidding process. Boycott sentiment peaked in the months before the tournament but collapsed once matches began, as entertainment value overwhelmed moral objections.

key_events.description: The Guardian reports 6,500 migrant worker deaths since Qatar won bid. European broadcaster boycott discussions. Amnesty International campaign intensifies.
Several European teams announce OneLove armband campaign. Denmark releases 'toned down' kits in protest. Boycott sentiment at peak in Northern Europe.
FIFA threatens sporting sanctions over OneLove armbands. Teams back down. Peak of boycott frustration — 48% support boycott in European polls.
Tournament begins November 20. Saudi Arabia stuns Argentina. Dramatic group stage matches. TV viewership surges globally despite boycott pledges.
Argentina wins dramatic final vs. France (penalties). Tournament hailed as one of the best ever on-pitch. Boycott movement effectively dissolved by entertainment value.

calibration_notes: Classic case of 'protest fatigue meets entertainment capture.' Boycott support followed an inverted U-curve — building steadily pre-tournament, then collapsing rapidly once exciting matches began. YouGov data showed European boycott support peaking at ~45-50% in October 2022, dropping to ~25% by tournament's end. Tests: moral conviction vs. entertainment/cultural pull, protest sustainability, media attention competition.
